commands.review_summary:
This PR updates the Gmail drafts delete help text and destructive-confirmation prompt to say deletion is permanent, adds a code comment, and adds a CHANGELOG entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source and generated-doc inspection show current main uses generic draft-delete wording, and the PR diff shows the generated command reference is not updated.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up rather than repair automation because real behavior proof is missing and the branch is dirty, while the remaining patch fixes are small and mechanical.

target.title: docs(gmail): clarify that drafts delete is permanent
target.body:
Fixes #656.

## Problem
`gog gmail drafts delete` is **permanent and irreversible** — the Gmail API's `users.drafts.delete` deletes the draft outright; drafts are not moved to Trash and there's no untrash path (`gog gmail trash` operates on message IDs only). But the command's help (`"Delete a draft"`) and confirmation prompt (`"delete gmail draft <id>"`) read like an ordinary, recoverable delete, so the irreversibility wasn't surfaced before confirming.

This is the documentation resolution the issue calls for: the Drafts API has no reversible delete to wire up, so the fix is to make the permanence explicit.

## Change
- Command help → `"Permanently delete a draft (not recoverable; drafts are not moved to Trash)"`.
- Destructive-confirmation prompt → `"permanently delete gmail draft <id> (not recoverable; drafts are not moved to Trash)"` (matching the existing `gmail batch delete` "permanently delete gmail messages" phrasing).
- Added a doc comment on `GmailDraftsDeleteCmd` explaining why it's irreversible.

No behavioural change — same API call, same `dryRunAndConfirmDestructive` guard; only the wording is clarified.

## Testing
`go test ./internal/cmd/` green (the dry-run e2e contract asserts the `gmail.drafts.delete` op, which is unchanged). `make fmt` + `make lint` (0 issues). CHANGELOG updated.
target.author: chrischall
